channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Achieving a Balanced Planet: The Critical Role of Digital Technology
item.description: The energy transition to a lower carbon future is the largest and most significant challenge ever contemplated or attempted in history. Digital technology plays a crucial role in ensuring the reliability and sustainability of today's energy systems.
item.dc:creator: Rakesh Jaggi, Fast Company

item.title: Santa tracker 2024 live: how to follow Santa with Google or Norad
item.description: Want the best Santa Tracker and to play some fun games through Norad or Google? We're live-blogging Santa's movements as he ventures out from the North Pole
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: This new rugged Panasonic 2-in-1 tablet with 13th-gen Intel processors, 5G connectivity, and a 12-inch QHD display is perfect for even the toughest conditions
item.description: The Panasonic TOUGHBOOK 33mk4 is built for demanding environments and offers enhanced performance for mobile workers in tough conditions.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Elon Musk’s xAI supercomputer gets 150MW power boost despite concerns over grid impact and local power stability
item.description: xAI gains 150MW power approval for its supercomputer, but locals fear impact on grid and future power availability.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Chat with Santa or find out if you are on the naughty list with these tech features
item.description: You can access the new (and limited!) Santa Chat feature through the ChatGPT app by starting a new chat, hitting the live chat button.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, NewsNation

item.title: Bluesky finds with growth comes growing pains — and bots
item.description: Bluesky’s growth trajectory — with a user base that has more than doubled since October — could make it a serious competitor to other social platforms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: We used Google’s AI to analyze 188 predictions of what’s in store for tech in 2025
item.description: At this time of year investment banks, advertising agencies, and seemingly every other business on the planet share their predictions on what is likely to unfold in the next 12 months. Journalists’ inboxes sag under the weight of unsolicited predictions for the year ahead.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Hallucinations to agents: Here’s how AI language models evolved in 2024
item.description: I research the intersection of artificial intelligence, natural language processing, and human reasoning as the director of the Advancing Human and Machine Reasoning lab at the University of South Florida.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: AI will eavesdrop on world's wildest places to track and help protect endangered wildlife
item.description: A biologist hid 350 audio monitors across Costa Rica’s tropical rainforests to spy on endangered spider monkeys in order to help protect them.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chris Christie predicts Elon Musk's influence over Trump will end
item.description: Former New Jersey Gov. Chris Christie (R) predicted in a Sunday interview that tech billionaire Elon Musk’s influence over President-elect Trump will end. “When does this end?” ABC News’s Jonathan Karl asked Christie on “This Week” of Musk’s influence on Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: These are the companies using AI-driven dynamic pricing the most - and the top users probably won't surprise you
item.description: Amazon leads dynamic pricing with 12.6 daily changes, but Best Buy changes its price only 2.6 times per day.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Albania to shut down TikTok for a year over violence, bullying
item.description: Prime Minister Edi Rama, speaking at a meeting with teachers and parents, said TikTok "would be fully closed for all. There will be no TikTok in the Republic of Albania." Rama said the shutdown would begin sometime next year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ex-OpenAI engineer who raised legal concerns about the technology he helped build has died
item.description: Suchir Balaji, a former OpenAI engineer and whistleblower who helped train the artificial intelligence systems behind ChatGPT and later said he believed those practices violated copyright law, has died, according to his parents and San Francisco officials.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: WordPress.org statement threatens possible shutdown for all of 2025
item.description: Automattic CEO Matt Mullenweg on Friday announced a shutdown of almost all services on WordPress.org, the open source project site that’s the home of the software, plugins, and the WordPress community, but was unclear on when the shutdown would end.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Never need an EV charging station again with these rooftop solar power panels
item.description: An inventor designed rooftop solar panels for a Tesla that draws solar energy while the car is parked, adding travel mileage without plugging in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cost-conscious repatriation strategies
item.description: In recent years, the cloud computing landscape has undergone a seismic shift. After fully embracing the potential of public cloud services, many companies are reevaluating their strategies and shifting workloads back to on-premises infrastructures.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Transparency, efficiency, innovation: The digital transformation of policing in Kazakhstan
item.description: In the era of digital transformation, public safety stands at a critical crossroads. Law enforcement agencies globally are under increasing scrutiny to enhance transparency, efficiency, and trust within their communities.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ireland’s AI data centers are sucking up too much of the country’s energy
item.description: Ireland is still heavily reliant on burning fossil fuels to generate electricity, despite a growing number of wind farms sprouting across the countryside. Further data center expansion threatens Ireland's goals to sharply cut planet-warming emissions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Slack adds Agentforce ‘hub’ for AI agents
item.description: Slack is adding a new way to access AI agents created on parent company Salesforce’s Agentforce platform, with a library of options available in the collaboration app. Agentforce, which launched in October, is a tool to build AI agents that answer questions and automate tasks for users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: From CAPTCHA to catastrophe: How fake verification pages are spreading malware
item.description: CAPTCHAs, which are used by websites to confirm whether users are people or bots, are harmless, but hackers are using them to infect PCs with malware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 wizardly ways to integrate web apps with Windows
item.description: While Windows is a powerful operating system for productivity, so much of what we do with work these days revolves around the web.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: You have a license to code
item.description: One of the coolest things about software development is that you don’t need anyone’s permission to do it. If you need an application for your business or personal use, there isn’t a single thing stopping you from learning to code and writing it yourself.
item.dc:creator: Info World
